---
title: "Elevation"
description: "How high Canada is — the distribution of land by elevation, and where the high ground lies"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json, base64
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map, relief_map

MAP_CONFIG = {"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True,
              "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]}
dist_csv = "data/geo/elevation_distribution.csv"
prov_geo = "data/geo/prov_2021.geojson"
elev_csv = "data/geo/elevation_by_prov.csv"
prov_names = "data/geo/statcan_prov_geography.csv"
```

## The shape of the land

Seen whole, Canada's surface resolves into a few great physiographic regions: the high, folded
**Western Cordillera** along the Pacific; the flat-then-rising **Interior Plains** of the
Prairies; the ancient, low **Canadian Shield** wrapped around Hudson Bay; the **lowlands** of
the St. Lawrence and Hudson Bay; and the worn **Appalachian** uplands of the east. The relief
map colours the land by elevation — green lowlands through tan and brown to the near-white high
ground of the Rockies, the Coast Mountains, and the Arctic ranges.

```{python}
#| echo: false
relief_png, relief_meta = "data/geo/elevation_relief.png", "data/geo/elevation_relief.json"
if os.path.exists(relief_png) and os.path.exists(relief_meta):
    uri = "data:image/png;base64," + base64.b64encode(open(relief_png, "rb").read()).decode()
    coords = json.load(open(relief_meta))["coordinates"]
    prov = json.load(open(prov_geo)) if os.path.exists(prov_geo) else None
    fig = relief_map(uri, coords, boundary_geojson=prov, boundary_color="#7c8088",
        boundary_width=0.5, center={"lat": 60, "lon": -96}, zoom=2.3,
        source_note="Source: Natural Resources Canada — Medium-Resolution DEM (MRDEM-30, CanElevation). Coarse national read, reprojected to Web Mercator; colour = elevation.")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("Relief image not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

## How high is Canada?

For all its mountains, Canada is a remarkably **low-lying** country. About **three-quarters of
its land sits below 500 metres**, and barely **half a percent rises above 2,000 metres** — the
high peaks of the western Cordillera and the Arctic make up a tiny share of the total area. The
chart shows how the country's land is distributed across elevation bands.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(dist_csv):
    d = pd.read_csv(dist_csv)
    fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
        y=d["band"], x=d["pct"], orientation="h", marker_color="#8c6d31",
        text=[f"{p:.0f}%" for p in d["pct"]], textposition="auto",
        hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f}% of land area<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        plot_bgcolor="white", height=380, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=44),
        xaxis=dict(title="Share of Canada's land area", ticksuffix="%", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
    fig.add_annotation(text="Source: Natural Resources Canada — Medium-Resolution Digital Elevation Model (MRDEM-30, CanElevation), coarse national read. Land area within province/territory boundaries.",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, xanchor="left", y=-0.18, showarrow=False,
        font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
else:
    print("Elevation data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

## Where the high ground lies

Median elevation makes the east–west tilt of the country plain: **British Columbia** and the
**Yukon** stand highest, the Prairies step down from the Rockies toward Manitoba, and the
Maritimes and the vast **Arctic lowlands** sit near sea level. (Median elevation is a coarse
summary of very large, varied regions.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(prov_geo) and os.path.exists(elev_csv):
    g = json.load(open(prov_geo))
    e = pd.read_csv(elev_csv, dtype={"pruid": str})
    nm = pd.read_csv(prov_names, dtype={"pruid": str})[["pruid", "name"]]
    e = e.merge(nm, on="pruid", how="left")
    fig = choropleth_map(g, e, "pruid", "median_elev", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="Median elev.", colorscale="YlOrBr", value_suffix=" m", value_fmt=",.0f",
        zoom=2.3, source_note="Source: Natural Resources Canada — MRDEM-30 (CanElevation). Median elevation within each province/territory, from a coarse national read (approximate).")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
```

## Download data

- [Land area by elevation band (CSV)](../data/geo/elevation_distribution.csv) — NRCan MRDEM-30
- [Median elevation by province (CSV)](../data/geo/elevation_by_prov.csv) — NRCan MRDEM-30
- [Relief image (PNG)](../data/geo/elevation_relief.png) — NRCan MRDEM-30, hypsometric tint
